chunks.text: Twenty four hundred years ago, in ancient Athens, there lived a philosopher named Socrates.
His philosophy existed only in the dialog he held with other people.
His student Plato reported, " The teacher said
' An unexamined life is not worth living.
'" Socrates kept asking people, " What is virtue?
He would claim that he himself did not know
and then question those who claimed to know.
His questions slowly shook the authority of those people.
Socrates was accused of corrupting the young and was condemned at his trial.
Friends urged him to flee, but he replied
" To be ethical is also to die correctly.
He drank the hemlock and died quietly.
But his question, over two thousand years
is still turned toward us now: " Do you examine your own life?
